description: Heey-Global org-wide Renovate preset. Repos inherit it with `"extends": ["local>Heey-Global/.github"]` so the shared policy (3-day stability window, vulnerability fast-track, h-teske assignment) lives in ONE place instead of being copy-pasted (and drifting) across every repo. This file is ALSO the config Renovate uses to manage the .github repo itself.

Digest cooldown coupling: the 3-day `minimumReleaseAge` below only protects updates that carry a datasource release timestamp (versions/tags). Digest updates (@sha256 image digests / pinned action SHAs) carry NO timestamp, so without an exemption Renovate parks them on `renovate/stability-days` forever. The `packageRules` below exempt digest/pinDigest from `minimumReleaseAge` so they leave that limbo. This is SAFE because the cooldown is re-imposed org-wide by the `digest-age` required workflow (Heey-Global/.github/.github/workflows/digest-age-check.yml, distributed via an org branch ruleset rule 'Require workflows to pass before merging'), which re-derives the same 3-day soak from each digest's authoritative upstream creation date — the date Renovate refuses to trust by design. The two are coupled: if that org ruleset were ever removed, this exemption would let un-soaked digests merge, so the gate must stay active wherever this preset is extended.

customManagers.description: Extract pinned npm package versions from `npm install -g pkg@version` lines inside reusable workflows. Renovate's stock github-actions manager only catches `uses:` action pins; inline package installs need this customManager to be visible. Annotation must sit directly above the install line — see `lockfile-lint.yml` for the canonical shape.

packageRules.description: Exempt digest updates (@sha256 image digests + action commit-SHA pins) from the 3-day minimumReleaseAge. Digests carry no datasource release timestamp, so the cooldown would otherwise park every digest-update PR on `renovate/stability-days: pending` forever — the PR never becomes mergeable even when the underlying image/commit is months old. The cooldown is re-imposed org-wide by the `digest-age` required workflow (an org branch ruleset rule pointing at Heey-Global/.github/.github/workflows/digest-age-check.yml), which re-derives the same 3-day soak from each digest's authoritative upstream creation date. KEEP COUPLED: removing this exemption while the org digest-age ruleset is inactive would let un-soaked digests merge.
